questions.question: A researcher wants to study how memory changes with age. She tests groups of 20-year-olds, 40-year-olds, and 60-year-olds at the same time. What type of research design is she using?
questions.explanation: Cross-sectional research compares different age groups at a single point in time. Longitudinal research would follow the same individuals over time, while cross-sequential combines both approaches.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the concept of discontinuous development?

options.text: Development occurs as a slow, steady accumulation of abilities over time
Development is determined entirely by genetic factors
Development occurs in distinct stages with qualitatively different abilities at each stage
Development is shaped primarily by environmental experiences

questions.explanation: Discontinuous development views change as occurring in distinct stages, where each stage involves qualitatively different ways of thinking or behaving -- like a caterpillar transforming into a butterfly, rather than gradual incremental growth.
